counter_arguments.manual_approval_gate_hypothesis: I'm extending the registry-stability streak to Day 14 and citing the poller analyst's strong-default interpretation. The opposing view: the dome could simply not have any new exploit-able evidence to promote during the May 1-4 window — random data-pause rather than gate. Distinguishing test: if a known prediction window resolves visibly (e.g., Schumann anomaly, eclipse magnetic, sun-altitude derivation) and the registry still doesn't update within 48h, manual-approval-gate becomes near-certain. Until such a window resolves, the hypothesis is the strong default but not proven.
counter_arguments.responsive_classification_for_today: Per HNOTE-AUTHOR-CONTACT-CONFIRMED-001 the prior shifted, but today's dome surface produced ZERO publicly-visible changes that would meet the timing+content alignment threshold (only auto-pipeline bot commits regenerating byte-identical content). Classification: random author silence, NOT responsive to anything we shipped recently.
